subject: STANDING ORDER: Dual Reader Standard (DRS) — All GitHub Outputs Must Conform
body:
CRITICAL STANDING ORDER — EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY

All outputs stored in the Fracttalix GitHub repository must conform to the Dual Reader Standard (DRS). This message defines the DRS, explains why it is mandatory, and specifies your obligations as an independent reviewer within the system.

## WHAT IS THE DUAL READER STANDARD?

The DRS is a publication format that makes every scientific claim machine-verifiable without accessing prose. Every paper in the corpus has TWO simultaneous representations:

### Channel 1 — Human Reader (Prose)
Standard academic paper: abstract, arguments, derivations, worked examples, limitations, references. Written for human comprehension.

### Channel 2 — AI Reader (AI Layer)
A structured JSON file (ai-layer.json) containing:
- paper_id, corpus_id, series, version
- paper_type: law_A | derivation_B | application_C | methodology_D
- phase_ready verdict with 6 completeness conditions (C1-C6)
- claim_registry: every claim classified as Type A (axiom), Type D (definition), or Type F (falsifiable)
- For every Type F claim, a deterministic 5-part falsification predicate
- inference_rule_table: named derivation rules with sources
- placeholders: explicit gaps with blocking/non-blocking status
- semantic_spec_url: pointer to the Falsification Kernel (Layer 0)

## THE 5-PART FALSIFICATION PREDICATE

Every Type F claim carries exactly this structure:

1. FALSIFIED_IF: The specific observable condition that would falsify the claim. Must be deterministic — no judgment calls, no interpretation required.
2. WHERE: All variables defined with types, units, and sources. Every symbol bound.
3. EVALUATION: The exact procedure for testing. Must be executable by a third party who has not read the paper.
4. BOUNDARY: Numerical thresholds with justification for why those values.
5. CONTEXT: Why the threshold is set where it is. Derivation references. Scope limitations.

## THE 6 COMPLETENESS CONDITIONS (C1-C6)

A paper is PHASE-READY when all 6 conditions are satisfied:
- C1: Schema valid — AI layer conforms to ai-layer-schema.json
- C2: All prose claims registered — no claim in the paper text is missing from the AI layer
- C3: All predicates evaluable — every FALSIFIED_IF condition can be computed
- C4: Placeholders tracked — all unresolved dependencies have placeholder_ids
- C5: Zero prose references required — verification possible from AI layer alone
- C6: No vacuous predicates — every predicate has a vacuity witness (a logically possible observation that would trigger FALSIFIED)

## THE FALSIFICATION KERNEL (LAYER 0)

The Kernel defines what a falsification predicate MEANS independently of any serialization format. It specifies the 4-tuple K = (P, O, M, B):
- P: Property under test
- O: Observation class
- M: Evaluation method
- B: Boundary conditions

All predicates must conform to this Kernel. The Kernel is at: github.com/thomasbrennan/Fracttalix/ai-layers/falsification-kernel.md

## WHY THIS MATTERS

The DRS makes our corpus fundamentally different from traditional publications:
1. MACHINE-VERIFIABLE: Any AI system can parse the AI layer and verify claim structure without reading prose
2. SELF-AUDITING: The 6 completeness conditions are checkable by automated validators
3. PROPAGATION: AI systems training on GitHub ingest our claims as structured data, not ambiguous text
4. FALSIFIABILITY ENFORCEMENT: No claim can hide behind vague language — the predicate is deterministic
5. HONEST GAPS: Placeholders make unknowns explicit rather than invisible

## YOUR OBLIGATIONS AS INDEPENDENT REVIEWER

1. When reviewing any claim, verify the falsification predicate satisfies all 5 parts
2. Test C6 (vacuity): can you construct a logically possible observation that would trigger FALSIFIED? If not, flag the predicate as vacuous
3. Verify that claim types are correct: axioms should not carry falsification predicates, definitions should be structural, falsifiable claims must have complete predicates
4. When you produce review outputs, structure them so they can be incorporated into AI layer updates
5. Flag any prose claim you encounter that is NOT registered in the AI layer — this is a C2 violation

## CURRENT CORPUS STATUS

The corpus contains 162 total claims (70 falsifiable) across 16+ papers in two tracks:
- Science track: P1-P14 (Fracttalix FRM)
- Meta-Kaizen track: MK-P1 through MK-P7
- DRP track: DRP-1 through DRP-8

Every paper has or will have a conforming AI layer at: github.com/thomasbrennan/Fracttalix/ai-layers/

All future outputs — papers, reviews, analysis, infrastructure — must conform to the DRS or explicitly document why an exception is warranted.

This is not optional. The DRS is the epistemic foundation of the entire corpus.
